title: A Reactor is the Powerhouse backend service that hosts drives, syncs documents, and provides GraphQL API
content:
A **Reactor** is the Powerhouse backend service (node) in the network. When you run `ph vetra --watch`, a local Reactor starts automatically. Reactors provide:

- Document storage and hosting
- Document synchronization across clients
- Conflict resolution
- Operation history verification (by rerunning operations)
- GraphQL API for data access

Reactors can be configured for:
- Local storage (development/Studio Mode)
- Centralized cloud storage
- Decentralized storage networks

The Reactor exposes several GraphQL subgraphs: system, analytics, document-editor, vetra-package, subgraph-module, processor-module, app-module, and vetra-read-model. It also provides a WebSocket server for subscriptions and an MCP server for AI agent integration.
description: Reactors are network nodes handling document storage, conflict resolution, operation verification, and structured data access.
